vulnerability.reference: Over-reliance on opaque CLIP embeddings without human-interpretable validation in safety-critical autonomous systems.
vulnerability.generated: Operators depend on anomaly detectors that cannot flag adversarial inputs mimicking clean topological persistence signatures.

impact.reference: Undetected adversarial inputs cause autonomous vehicle collisions, eroding public trust in AI safety.
impact.generated: Safety incidents and operational failures result from adversarial inputs triggering dangerous autonomous actions without operator alert.

dose_response.reference: Adversarial perturbations exploit multimodal alignment gaps to evade detection by mimicking clean topological signatures; in deployment of CLIP-based anomaly detection in autonomous systems, this exposes operators to undetected adversarial inputs, leading to autonomous vehicle collisions and eroded public trust.
dose_response.generated: Adversarial perturbations exploiting multimodal alignment gaps to mimic clean topological signatures, through operators relying on CLIP-based anomaly detectors, trigger dependence on detectors unable to flag such inputs, leading to safety incidents and operational failures from unalerted dangerous autonomous actions.
